meta.appDescription: Track income, spending, investments, and rental properties. Data stored locally.

navigation.confirmNewDraft: Reset the draft? Changes not saved to a project will be lost.
navigation.themeCycle: Theme: tap to cycle system, light, then dark

savedProjects.autoSaveDraft: Draft: load a project to attach changes to a named snapshot.
savedProjects.confirmLoad: Replace the on-screen data with this project? Unsaved changes not stored in another project will be lost (the current draft will be overwritten).

dataPage.title: Data entry
dataPage.subtitle: Enter your income, spending, rental properties, and investments.
dataPage.footer: Data is stored locally on your device. Use “Projects” in the top bar to save or load named scenarios. JSON export/import below.

dataJson.confirmImport: Replace all on-screen data with this file? Current data will be lost unless you exported it elsewhere.

error.invalid_json: This file is not valid JSON (syntax error).
error.not_object: JSON must be an object { … }, not an array or plain text.
error.unexpected_shape: This JSON does not look like a Finance Pilot backup (expected keys such as revenues, fixed expenses, annex budgets, etc.).

rental.title: Rental properties
rental.maintenanceHintPct: % of rent set aside each month for upkeep
rental.maintenanceHintFixed: Fixed amount set aside for upkeep
rental.otherChargesHint: Fees, agent, accountant, external management, etc. — in euros (monthly or yearly) or as a % of monthly rent received.

investments.title: Investments and savings
investments.placeholder: E.g. stocks & shares ISA, crypto, bond…

estimations.title: Forecasts and projections
estimations.subtitle: See your finances at a glance and project your wealth over time.
estimations.noInvestmentsBody: Add investments on the Data page to see how your portfolio could grow over time.
estimations.footerDisclaimer: Forecasts use the returns you enter; they are not a guarantee of future performance.

comparaison.title: Compare projects
comparaison.subtitle: Compare projected performance over several years: portfolio value from investments (same method as the Forecasts page), alongside monthly balances and net rental income for each scenario.
comparaison.alertNoProjectsBody: You can compare the draft to itself to try the screen, or save projects from the “Projects” menu to compare stored scenarios.
comparaison.alertSameBody: Both lines use the same data. Pick two different sources for a meaningful comparison.
comparaison.scenarioADesc: Data used for the first line
comparaison.scenarioBDesc: Data used for the second line
comparaison.projectionDesc: Estimated value of investment lines (contributions and growth at the rate entered for each line).
comparaison.noInvestmentsBoth: Neither scenario has investments: add investment lines on the Data page to see a projection.
comparaison.gapHint: Positive if scenario A is ahead of B.
comparaison.tableDesc: Portfolio value and gap (A − B) for each year in the horizon.
comparaison.footerDisclaimer: Illustrative projections only: they use the returns you entered and do not predict real markets.

schedule.hint: For monthly tracking: category, mode, and day.
schedule.categoryNoMatch: No saved category matches. Type a name to create it below.
schedule.manual: Manual (transfer or payment you must initiate)
schedule.automatic: Automatic (direct debit on a set day)

advancedFinance.title: Monthly cashflow
advancedFinance.subtitle: As of today: manual transfers, upcoming automatic debits, and spend by category (scheduled fixed charges & annex budgets).
advancedFinance.empty: No lines have scheduling turned on. On the Data page, open a fixed charge or annex budget, tick “Enable monthly scheduling”, pick manual vs automatic and the day of month, then save. If you leave category blank, the line label is used.
advancedFinance.upcomingTitle: Automatic debits still due this month
advancedFinance.upcomingDesc: Debits still due this month (including today if it is the due day).
advancedFinance.manualDesc: One row per scheduling category: the amount is the sum of monthly equivalents. “Details” opens the lines in that group (tick “Done” per line). Checkmarks reset at the start of each month.
advancedFinance.detailNoEntity: This line no longer exists in your data.
advancedFinance.resetMonthConfirm: Clear all “done” checkmarks for the current month?
advancedFinance.pastAutoTitle: Automatic debits already taken this month

errors.financeContext: useFinanceData must be used inside a FinanceDataProvider
